steps.title: Substitute u = 8 - 2x to simplify
steps.content:
The expression $(8 - 2x)$ appears in both terms on both sides of the equation. Rather than expanding everything, let $u = 8 - 2x$ and rewrite the equation:

$$6u + 3 = 5u + 19$$

steps.title: Solve for u
steps.content:
Collect $u$ terms on the left and constants on the right:

$$6u - 5u = 19 - 3$$

$$u = 16$$

Since $u = 8 - 2x$, we have $8 - 2x = 16$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
